reasoning: The Lean statement encodes the informal claim as an iff: given ¬n.Prime, (n-1)! ≡ 0 [MOD n] ↔ n ≠ 4. The backward direction captures the main assertion "composite n, n≠4 ⇒ (n-1)! ≡ 0 (mod n)." The forward direction corresponds to the "except when n=4" clause, which naturally asserts that n=4 is genuinely exceptional (i.e., (3)! = 6 ≢ 0 mod 4, which is true). The ℕ+ type and ¬n.Prime hypothesis match "n is not prime" (including n=1 trivially, since 1 ≡ 0 mod 1). This is the standard interpretation of the classical Wilson-converse exercise in Ireland-Rosen.
